photo_captions: Barfleur (postcard — aerial view of Barfleur harbour)
pages.notes: Page contains a glued-in colour postcard of Barfleur (the word 'BARFLEUR' is printed on the postcard). Below/beside the postcard is a handwritten caption 'John's 17th birthday.' in blue ink. The right-hand margin shows partial text that is the 3% gutter overlap with the right page (B_0047_R); that text is not transcribed here to avoid duplication. No handwritten page number visible in the top-left corner.

pages.full_text:
Sunday 29th August.
        9.45. we set off, Simon, Eve, John, Charles, Melody 14 & Charlotte 13, to __Bath__ to Camp with David. We arrived & found their van at the Marina Campsite [illegible] but they had gone out. The 4 children played 4 in a Row & chess at the table, while Simon & Eve went out for a walk by the river. When we got back Eve had to yell "John, John Kirby!" several times before Charles came & let us in. Everyone was laughing.
        We had a very slow start to lunch with extremely strong cider provided by David & just as we started to carve the chicken at 2 pm the heavens opened with 2 heavy rain showers. We left the food under a parasol & sheltered in the vans. Finally we got fed.
        Soon after, at [?3].45. (!) we set off walking into Bath. Charles had hurt his back falling off the swing in the playground.
pages.notes: Handwritten page number 45 in top-right corner. The time '3.45' has a strikethrough on the 3 and '4' written above — likely meaning 4.45; transcribed as [?3].45. The diary mentions children's ages (Melody 14, Charlotte 13) which could help narrow the year but names/ages are not independently verifiable here. 'Sunday 29th August' fell on: 2004 and 2010 within the volume's 2003-2019 range — year assigned tentatively as 2004 but confidence is low without further context. The '##' or similar symbol after 'Marina Campsite' is unclear — transcribed as [illegible]. Volume B covers 2003-2019; position in volume (page 47 of 373) suggests early in the volume, consistent with 2003-2004.
